data.title: MGT Impact Solutions, LLC announced that it has received $350 million in funding from Ares Management Corporation
data.description:
MGT Impact Solutions, LLC announced that it has received $350 million in a round of funding on December 3, 2025. The transaction included participation from new investor, Ares Management Corporation. The company has issued convertible preferred stock.

The round is raised at a post money valuation of $1,250 million.

data.description:
Motorola (MSI) isn't just flip-phone nostalgia anymore. From car radios to AI-powered body cams and license-plate readers, it's become a quiet powerhouse in policing and defense. CEO Greg Brown joined Bloomberg Open Interest on how he built a nearly 1,000% stock-return machine over 14 years and where it goes next."

data.title: 'Everything's gone': Hong Kong family returns to site of deadly fire
data.description:
STORY: :: 'Everything's gone', says one resident returning to the charred site of Hong Kong's high-rise inferno 

:: Hong Kong/ December 3, 2025

Leung, Wang Fuk Court resident: "It all happened within just an hour or two. I stood there watching as one block after another went up in flames, my legs felt so weak I could hardly stand."

:: November 26, 2025

:: File

Leung: "Now, whenever I close my eyes, I see that day again. I can't sleep. I just can't. I think about my home, how I couldn't bring a single thing with me. I just walked out like that. Everything's gone. Everything I built over decades has vanished. I was supposed to live out my later years there peacefully, spending free time with my husband walking along the waterfront park near our home. Those moments are all just memories now." 

Bonnie Leung, Leung's daughter: "We also hope the truth will come out -- whether there were hidden hands behind this, corruption, or any improper dealings. Everyone knows this might not be a simple accident; there were too many human factors involved."

Leung and her husband had called their flat in the housing complex home for about 40 years before the devastating fire struck last Wednesday (November 26). Purchased as a second-hand property from her younger sister for over $128,000 (HK$1 million), the 500-square-foot apartment had been where Leung planned to peacefully spend her later years with her husband.

Pictures shared by their daughter Bonnie showed their home's living room and a view of the area from their balcony. Reuters was able to confirm the location of the photos.

The death toll in Hong Kong's deadliest fire in decades hit at least 159 on Wednesday, with police revealing that victims included an infant and a 97-year-old, as they continued their search for remains in the burnt-out apartment complex. Some 30 people are still missing.

All residents of the Wang Fuk Court complex, numbering over 4,000, have been displaced by the city's worst blaze in decades and many are being put up in temporary housing.

Copper Continues to Surge – Now Above $11,500
data.description:
The price of copper continues to climb to new record highs on Wednesday.

A weaker dollar and supply concerns are fueling the rally.

At the time of writing, the copper price is up 3.5 percent to $11,534 per ton.

JP Morgan analysts predict that copper prices could rise to $12,500 during the first half of next year, according to Bloomberg.

AerCap Holdings N.V. announces an Equity Buyback for $1,000 million worth of its shares.
data.description:
AerCap Holdings N.V. (NYSE:AER) announces a share repurchase program. Under the program, the company will repurchase up to $1,000 million worth of its shares. The program will be funded using company?s cash on hand and cash generated from operations.

The program will run through June 30, 2026.

data.title: Italy to seek 5 billion euros in damages from ArcelorMittal over ILVA steelworks
data.description:
MILAN, Dec 3 (Reuters) - The commissioners of Italy's former ILVA steel plants will launch a 5-billion-euro ($5.83 billion) damages claim against ArcelorMittal, Industry Minister Adolfo Urso said on Wednesday, as the government seeks a new buyer for the steelmaker.

Acciaierie d'Italia (ADI), previously known as ILVA, was placed under government administration in early 2024, ending the ownership of ArcelorMittal, the world's second-largest steelmaker, and with the aim of finding new private investors.

"Extraordinary maintenance is absolutely necessary and stems from the state of total neglect and decay in which the plants were left by ArcelorMittal," Urso told parliament in announcing the damages claim.

ADI, which is struggling to maintain production amid rising energy costs and weak demand, is a major headache for Italian Prime Minister Giorgio Meloni because its closure would have major knock-on effects for the country's manufacturing sector.

In September, the steelmaker said it had received 10 bids for the plant, but that only two of the bidders - Azerbaijan's Baku Steel Company working with Azerbaijan Investment Company, and India's Jindal Steel International - were interested in purchasing all of the company's assets.

($1 = 0.8578 euros)

(Reporting by Sara Rossi, editing by Gavin Jones)

German, French, Spanish ministers to meet on FCAS on December 11, says source
data.description:
BERLIN, Dec 3 (Reuters) - Defence ministers from Germany, France and Spain are set to meet on December 11 to discuss FCAS, Europe's next-generation fighter jet project, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters on Wednesday.

The 100-billion-euro ($117 billion) Future Combat Air System (FCAS), floated more than eight years ago, has been mired in disputes between the companies involved over workshare and prized technology.

Bloomberg first reported the planned meeting.

At stake is the next phase of plans to deliver a fighter flanked by drones for France, Germany and Spain by 2040, mirroring a UK-Italian-Japanese initiative called GCAP.

However, disagreements between France's Dassault Aviation and Airbus mean the project has hit an impasse.

Last month, French President Emmanuel Macron and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz discussed the project in a bid to try to make progress.

($1 = 0.8574 euros)

(Reporting by Sabine Siebold Writing by Madeline ChambersEditing by Kirsti Knolle and Ludwig Burger)
